title: rosa canina subvar. cariotii
intro: Rosa canina subvar. cariotii, also known as the Cario's Dog Rose or the Dwarf Dog Rose, is a subvariety of the Rosa canina plant, a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. cariotii are attributed to its rich content of vitamins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to support eye health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, as well as its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease and improve digestion. Rosa canina subvar. cariotii can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which can be consumed as a tea, supplement, or added to topical creams and ointments.
